source: User request on 2026-05-12 after simplification: settings should live in local web app header top-right cog and map CodeWiki config options.
summary: Accepted Control Room Settings direction: add a header-right cog that opens a settings page mapping `.codewiki/config.json` options, without restoring Settings as a left-rail primary section.

diff_table.current_state: Simplified Control Room left rail excludes Settings; settings are only described as contextual command-palette or maintenance access.
diff_table.desired_state: Keep left rail simplified, but add a conventional header-right cog that opens a Settings page/panel in the web Control Room.
diff_table.rationale: The local web app has enough space and user expectations for a settings affordance without bloating primary navigation.

diff_table.current_state: Control Room does not expose `.codewiki/config.json` options in a structured UI.
diff_table.desired_state: Settings page maps current config options into grouped, source-backed rows with option path, current value, purpose/category, and read-only/editability cue.
diff_table.rationale: Users need visibility into CodeWiki configuration without reading raw JSON, while preserving config file as source truth.

diff_table.current_state: Config edits are not part of the simplified Control Room implementation.
diff_table.desired_state: First slice is read-only or clearly marks editability; semantic writes remain future/API-gated unless an existing safe update path exists.
diff_table.rationale: Avoid hidden UI-only truth or unsafe config mutation while making config understandable.

accepted_decisions.summary: Settings should be in the web Control Room header top-right as a cog, not left rail.
Settings maps `.codewiki/config.json` options into grouped UI rows.
Initial settings mapping should preserve `.codewiki/config.json` as source truth and avoid unsafe hidden writes.

assumptions: The cog may open an in-app settings page/modal while keeping Status/Product/System/Board/Graph left rail unchanged.
The first implementation can be read-only with editability labels; actual config editing can be added later if needed.
non_goals: Do not reintroduce Settings as a permanent left-rail section.
Do not create UI-only config state.
Do not implement broad config editing without a safe API path.
